title: Fiery Vietnamese Tofu Pho
description: A smoky, pillowy bowl of tofu pho fortified with coconut milk and a fiery kick, perfect for festive temple gatherings.
instructions: Heat the oil in a large pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add minced garlic and sliced ginger, sauté until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Stir in smoked paprika and toasted white pepper, cook for 30 seconds to release aromas.
Pour in vegetable stock and bring to a gentle boil.
Add vegetarian fish sauce and chili paste, whisk until dissolved.
Reduce heat and let the broth simmer for 10 minutes to meld flavors.
While the broth simmers, soak rice noodles in warm water for 5 minutes, then drain.
Fold in coconut milk, stir until fully incorporated and the broth is silky.
Add cubed tofu and simmer for another 5 minutes until tofu is heated through.
Ladle the hot broth and noodles into bowls, garnish with fresh herbs, lime wedges, bean sprouts, and a drizzle of chili paste if desired.
